shower.thought: We assume silence means agreement, but it might just mean someone stopped caring enough to fight.
Every conversation you have is the only version of that conversation that will ever exist.
You can't remember most of your life, which means you've already forgotten more than you'll ever know.
Nostalgia is just your brain editing out all the parts that were actually boring.
The people who say they "don't care what others think" usually care very much—just about different people.
We call it a "guilty pleasure" but the guilt is often the only reason it feels pleasurable.
You've probably had the same brilliant idea as someone else, but they just told someone about it first.
The scariest part of failure isn't falling—it's that nobody's watching, so nothing really changes.
Most of what you're anxious about never happens, but some of what you were anxious about last year actually did, which proves your anxiety wasn't wrong, just inefficient.
You can't be bored and curious at the same time, which is why boredom feels like the opposite of intelligence.
If a tree falls in the forest with nobody around, it still makes a sound—but more importantly, nobody has to pretend to care about your story about it.
The problem with being honest is everyone assumes you're being honest about everything, even the things you're not.
We're all just trying to buy time until we figure out what we're doing, and some people are just better at looking confident while doing it.
Your phone knows you better than you know yourself, which might be why it's so good at making you feel bad.
The people you envy on social media are probably envying someone else at that exact moment.
Wisdom isn't knowing the answer—it's knowing which questions have already destroyed other people.
You experience time moving faster as you get older because each year becomes a smaller percentage of your life, which means you're literally correct, but it doesn't make it less depressing.
The opposite of love isn't hate—it's indifference, which is why bad publicity is better than no publicity.
Most people don't actually want to change; they want permission to stay the same while feeling like they're trying.
A conversation is over when both people run out of things to say at the same time, which is why awkward silence exists.

motivational.thought: The only way out is through. — Robert Frost
Your limitations are often just borrowed beliefs. — Unknown
Growth whispers; regret screams. Choose to listen early. — Unknown
A problem is just a solution waiting for someone stubborn enough to find it. — Unknown
The person you're becoming is worth the discomfort you're feeling now. — Unknown
Comparison is the thief of progress. Compete only with who you were yesterday. — Unknown
You don't rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems. — Unknown
The work you avoid is the work that will define you. Do it anyway. — Unknown
Consistency beats intensity. Show up on the days you don't feel like it. — Unknown
Your future self is watching you right now. Make them proud. — Unknown
The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is today. — Ancient Proverb
Fear is the tax you pay for dreams worth having. — Unknown
Most people fail not because they aim too high, but because they quit too early. — Unknown
What we fear doing most is usually what we most need to do. — Tim Ferriss
Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most. — Unknown
The obstacle is the way. — Marcus Aurelius
Progress over perfection. Every day. — Unknown
You are capable of so much more than your comfort zone will ever show you. — Unknown
The same boiling water that softens the potato hardens the egg. It's about what you're made of. — Unknown
Your only real competition is the person you were yesterday. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought: I told my friend he had to stop making assumptions about people. He assumed I was right.
My grandfather said, "Life's too short to hold grudges." He's been dead for three years, so he was onto something.
I'm reading a book on the history of glue. I can't put it down.
My dating profile says I'm "emotionally unavailable." My therapist says it says "emotionally bankrupt," but she's not allowed to use my phone.
I asked my boss why I wasn't getting a raise. He said the economy was tough. I said so was my childhood, and I still showed up.
They told me to think outside the box. I did. Turns out the box was the only thing keeping me from falling apart.
My family says I'm too cynical. I said, "That's rich coming from people who microwave fish at work."
I told my doctor my back was killing me. He said, "At least something in your life is doing its job."
My therapist asked if I had any suicidal thoughts. I said, "Only on weekdays, so basically always."
I asked my father why he never told me I was adopted. He said, "Would you have believed me?"
My girlfriend left me because I was too negative. I told her it would never work out anyway.
My friend said he was going to cut out toxic people from his life. He didn't call me afterward, so I assume it's working.
I used to be addicted to soap. But I'm clean now.
My ex texted me, "I miss you." I said, "You have to aim better."
The doctor told me I had six months to live. When I couldn't pay the bill, he said, "Make it a year."
I told my kid that Santa wasn't real. She asked if Dad was either.
My therapist asked what my biggest fear was. I said, "Becoming my parents." She said, "Too late, that happened during our second session."
I joined a gym to get healthier. Turns out my biggest health risk was actually going to the gym.
My wife accused me of being selfish. I said, "How dare you talk about me like that."
They say money is the root of all evil. I have no money and I'm still evil, so I guess I'm just naturally gifted.
